data.title: Sprint orchestrator launches parallel sub-agents that modify the same files — source files excluded from conflict detection
data.description:
## What happened

In a host project sprint, the orchestrator launched parallel sub-agents that both modified the same source files:
- agents e115-2886 and 0d6a-f442 both modified data.fetch.test.ts
- agents e115-2886 and a6e2-454e both modified data.write.test.ts

The orchestrator logged: 'Note: agents ... both modify ... Will need to handle file overlap in post-batch. Waiting for completion.' — meaning the overlap was detected but NOT prevented. The agents were launched in parallel despite known file conflicts, deferring resolution to post-batch instead of preventing it.

## Expected behavior

The NxN pairwise overlap matrix (Phase 3 Batch Planning in debug-everything SKILL.md, and sprint-next-batch.sh) should detect write-write conflicts BEFORE dispatching sub-agents and defer the lower-priority agent to the next batch. Source files (test files, implementation files) should NOT be excluded from conflict detection.

## Root cause hypothesis

The sprint-next-batch.sh file overlap detection may be excluding test files or only checking files_likely_modified from analyze-file-impact.py, missing cases where the actual test/source files are the conflict. Alternatively, the conflict detection ran but the orchestrator proceeded anyway (logging a note instead of deferring).

## Fix needed

1. Investigate whether sprint-next-batch.sh and/or the sprint orchestrator's batch planning actually prevents parallel dispatch on file overlap, or merely logs it
2. Ensure source and test files are included in conflict detection (not just production source files)
3. If conflict is detected pre-dispatch, the lower-priority agent MUST be deferred to the next batch — logging a note and proceeding is not sufficient
